market_note: Six federal agencies must finalize GENIUS Act stablecoin rules by July 18; stablecoin market cap posted its biggest monthly drop in nearly four years in June even as transaction volume hit a record $1.79T.

stablecoin_items.headline: GENIUS Act rules due July 18
stablecoin_items.summary: Six federal agencies are finalizing stablecoin rules under the GENIUS Act; payment platforms like Stripe and Block must now charter a stablecoin bank or exit the market.

stablecoin_items.headline: Circle wins OCC trust bank charter
stablecoin_items.summary: Circle received an OCC charter on July 10, letting it manage USDC reserves directly; shares surged as competition with Tether intensifies.

stablecoin_items.headline: Stablecoin market cap posts biggest drop in 4 years
stablecoin_items.summary: Total stablecoin market cap fell $7.7B in June, led by USDT and USDC declines, even as adjusted transaction volume hit a record $1.79T, up 63% month over month.

stablecoin_items.headline: Ripple secures full MiCA authorization
stablecoin_items.summary: Luxembourg's CSSF granted Ripple full MiCA CASP authorization on July 6, enabling regulated stablecoin services across all 30 EEA countries.

hack_items.headline: Aptos flaw could have exposed $70B
hack_items.summary: White-hat firm Hexens found a critical Aptos flaw with a near-90% attack success rate that could have hit stablecoins and cross-chain bridges; a validator patch shipped before public disclosure, so no funds were lost.

hack_items.headline: 'Ill Bloom' exploit drains $5M+ from wallets
hack_items.summary: Attackers exploited a vulnerability dubbed 'Ill Bloom' to drain more than $5 million from cryptocurrency wallets in recent days.

hack_items.headline: H1 2026 hack losses top $950M
hack_items.summary: Crypto platforms suffered 207 publicly reported attacks in H1 2026 totaling roughly $955M-$972M in losses, with North Korea-linked hackers responsible for about 66% of stolen funds.

hack_items.headline: Humanity Protocol breach traced to malware
hack_items.summary: June's Humanity Protocol hack, which cost over $30M, stemmed from private keys backed up on a malware-infected developer machine.
